items.text: Post-delete notification/cache refresh is bounded; git worktree removal remains authoritative; timeout returns success with warning.
Pending-delete cleanup is bounded per item; timed-out item is retained, actual failed/timeout attempts increment, and later queued items continue.
In-use skips do not consume retry attempts; actual failed deletes and timeouts do.
Already-missing pending-delete paths are cleared without retrying forever.
Pending-delete retry cap is enforced at max 5 attempts unless an explicit operator cleanup uses force-attempt semantics.
`/adv-cleanup` reports worktree drift groups and does not delete worktrees, even with `--execute`.
Spec/docs/tests reflect the bounded cleanup law and full verification passes.

rows.evidence: Post-delete notification/cache refresh bounded in plugin/src/tools/worktree/index.ts; verified by index-delete.test.ts signal timeout test and full targeted suite.
rows.notes: Git worktree removal remains authoritative; notification timeout returns success with warning.

rows.evidence: Pending-delete cleanup bounded per item in drainPendingDeletes; verified by index-delete.test.ts timed-out first item continues to later item test.
rows.notes: Timed-out item retained, attempts incremented, later queued item processed.

rows.evidence: In-use skip path in drainPendingDeletes verified by index-delete.test.ts does-not-consume-retry-attempts test.
rows.notes: Actual failures and timeouts increment attempts; in-use skip retains without increment.

rows.evidence: Already-missing pending-delete paths clear in drainPendingDeletes; verified by index-delete.test.ts clears pending deletes whose worktree path is already gone.
rows.notes: No infinite retry for missing paths.

rows.evidence: MAX_PENDING_DELETE_ATTEMPTS=5 and forceAttempts retry-cap bypass verified by index-delete.test.ts retry-cap tests; review remediation added proof that forceAttempts does not force dirty deletion.
rows.notes: Manual cleanup can retry after cap while preserving dirty/unmerged safety.

rows.evidence: /adv-cleanup and adv-cleanup skill document report-only worktree drift behavior; adv-cleanup-contract-assets tests verify drift groups and non-destructive --execute behavior.
rows.notes: Cleanup report-only behavior remains documented and tested.

rows.evidence: Spec/docs/tests updated for rq-worktreeBoundedCleanup01. Verification passed: pnpm test -- src/tools/worktree/index-delete.test.ts (script ran full suite: 237 passed, 1 skipped), pnpm run check, pnpm run build, eslint, prettier, and strict validation with only NO_DELTAS warning.
rows.notes: Worktree-lifecycle docs mirror metadata aligned with spec JSON during review remediation.

acceptanceCriteria: Post-delete notification/cache refresh is bounded; git worktree removal remains authoritative; timeout returns success with warning.
Pending-delete cleanup is bounded per item; timed-out item is retained, actual failed/timeout attempts increment, and later queued items continue.
In-use skips do not consume retry attempts; actual failed deletes and timeouts do.
Already-missing pending-delete paths are cleared without retrying forever.
Pending-delete retry cap is enforced at max 5 attempts unless an explicit operator cleanup uses force-attempt semantics.
`/adv-cleanup` reports worktree drift groups and does not delete worktrees, even with `--execute`.
Spec/docs/tests reflect the bounded cleanup law and full verification passes.
